comments.author: @VILJL
comments.text:
Eloy Room makes 15 saves to help Curacao get first World Cup point
If 15 saves seems like a lot, it's because it is. And it is almost unheard of for World Cup competition, and especially in a match that does not include extra time.
The 15 saves are the second-most ever in a World Cup match since save records were first officially tracked in 1966, trailing only the 16 made by USA's Tim Howard against Belgium in 2014 in a Round of 16 match.

title: DOGSO or Not?! Inter Miami Penalty Chaos & RED CARD Debate | Instant Replay

comments.author: @scottgluck2953
comments.text: I would love an explanation how extending St. Clair's left arm to take out the attacking player by his head/throat is playing the ball there, Wiebe.  He STARTS OFF attempting to play the ball, but when the touch gets taken over his head, St. Clair just chops off the attacker's head with his arm.  Any other player on the pitch strikes a player in the head/neck like St. Clair did and that's a Red for Endangering the Safety of an Opponent.  He COULD have brought his hand straight down to avoid the foul, but instead swings his arm out wide and strikes the attacker in the head for the foul.  That's gotta be a red every time.

comments.author: @bbot16
comments.text: St. Clair did not play the ball. What are you talking about?

comments.author: @luishernandezzzz
comments.text: andrew wiebe "the keeper hesitates and then clearly fouls" that sounds like he didn't play the ball then. It is clear as day that its dogso.

comments.author: @BrooklynDavis-y7v
comments.text:
I’ve seen some people complain about durkins simulation call not being added here. I must say, there’s not much for Wiebe to debate. This isn’t a reffing fails compilation, it’s a controversial call series. 

It’s such an obvious reffing blunder I don’t mind it being left out. Maybe you could say that the MLS is trying to hide the horrendous calls, but I think it’s just as much that the call itself was just an obvious wrong decision. (I’m an stl fan)

comments.author: @GasanAlakbarov
comments.text:
Dear Andy,
It is incredibly hard to accept that the time has come to say goodbye to our Working-Class Hero. When you arrived in 2017 for just £8 million from Hull City, nobody outside of Liverpool knew what was coming. Nine years and nine trophies later, you leave us as an undisputed legend and one of the greatest left-backs to ever wear the red shirt.
Thank you for the endless miles you ran down that left flank. Thank you for the ferocious tackles, the pinpoint crosses, and the unmatched passion that defined the spirit of this team. We will never forget you pressing the entire Manchester City defense by yourself, or how you stood up for your teammates whenever they needed a fighter. You gave us your heart and soul in every single second on the pitch.
Beyond your world-class talent, thank you for showing us what it means to be a true family man. Watching the emotional video of Rachel and your beautiful children saying "To Daddy..." reminded us of the incredible human being you are behind the scenes. Balancing the immense pressure of playing for Liverpool while being an exemplary father is your greatest achievement. You proved that humility and greatness can live in the same man.
From Scotland to Merseyside, you became one of our own. You embraced this city, its people, and our values with everything you had.
As you prepare to walk out of Anfield for the last time, know that your name is permanently etched into our history. The Kop will forever sing your song, and your legacy of hard work, grit, and joy will inspire generations of Reds to come.
Go smash your next chapter, Robbo. But never forget that this city will always be a home for you and your family.
You will always be a Red. And You Will Never Walk Alone.
With deepest respect and eternal gratitude,
From Gasan Alakbarov, and Every Single Red Around the World. 🔴🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿

title: Mo Salah's Anfield Goodbye with Makka & Kayan | 'It will always be the Reds' ❤️

comments.author: @GasanAlakbarov
comments.text:
Dear Mo,
It is hard to find the words to say goodbye to a man who rewrote our history. When you first arrived in 2017, we knew we were signing a talented winger. We had no idea we were welcoming a King.
Thank you for 257 moments of pure magic. Thank you for the breathtaking solo goals, the iconic arms-wide celebrations in front of the Kop, and the relentless hunger that brought us back to the absolute pinnacle of world football. Because of you, we finally conquered the Premier League again. Because of you, Madrid and June 1st, 2019, will live in our hearts forever.
Beyond the trophies and the golden boots, thank you for sharing your family with us. Watching Makka and Kayan grow up on the Anfield turf—from Makka scoring in an empty net in 2018 to both of them walking the pitch with you one last time—has been a beautiful privilege. Hearing that your daughters told you, "We will always support the Reds," brought tears to our eyes. It proved what we already knew: Liverpool didn't just buy a player; we adopted a family.
You gave this city your prime, your passion, and your poetry on the pitch. You became a symbol of hope, humility, and joy for millions of fans across the globe.
As you walk away from Anfield for the final time, remember that you never truly leave. The songs will still be sung. The memories will never fade. You are woven into the very fabric of this football club.
Go conquer your next chapter, Mo. But wherever your journey takes you, remember the promise your daughters made, and the truth we will always hold dear:
You will always be a Red. And You Will Never Walk Alone.
With endless love and gratitude,
From Gasan Alakbarov, and Every Single Red Around the World. 🔴👑

comments.text:
0:42
No, that's a handball. Come on.

comments.author: @shuffluffugus3736
comments.text: Jesus, c'mon Andrew, there is no way that's a foul by Kossa-Rienzi

comments.author: @IjustwatchOlO
comments.text: No delay on the last one wtf?

comments.author: @canalphi2673
comments.text:
1 - is not a penalty because, yes, his arms are open, but it is part of the natural movement, as the handball rule says, “A player is considered to have made their body unnaturally bigger when the position of their hand/arm is not a consequence of, or justifiable by, the player’s body movement for that specific situation." His arms being open is completely justifiable considering he was falling. 
2 - It's the same case as the first and by rule is not a penalty; again, no one is making their body "unnaturally bigger' without being part of a player's natural body movement.
3 - The hug happens, but it's soft; that kind of thing happens in every corner kick. The player did not fall because of the hug, but because he threw himself into the ground, so he had no real impact, so not a penalty. 
4 - Very soft touch; you can argue the t-shirt was pulled, but I would not call it, and I doubt the majority of refs would.
5 - Right decision; the same thing happened in a Libertadores final with a manager called Cuca, same result: red card.

comments.author: @glennmitchell9107
comments.text: Why does FIFA allow so many players in the goal box or even in the penalty box during free kicks?  Packing that many players in such a small area is designed to create fouls and injuries.  Even when a goal is awarded off a free kick, it seems one or more fouls could be called.  And then there is all the injury time with one or more players laid out on the pitch holding their heads or grabbing their backs.  Consider this rule change.  During free kicks, excepting the goalie, only allow two players from each team in the goal box and only allow two players from each team in the penalty area outside of the goal box.

comments.author: @danwenger1113
comments.text:
The reason we didn't really see the efforts of Martin Ødegaard in previous games was because he was doing double the work — both as an attacking midfielder and almost covering for Zubimendi in defensive midfield.

A lot of his work rate dragged him deeper to help with the build-up, and whenever he tried to play offensively, Zubimendi’s constant back passes pulled him backwards again.

Since Mikel Arteta unlocked the midfield with Myles Lewis-Skelly, the team would look tactically stronger if he drops Eberechi Eze and plays a Skelly–Rice–Ødegaard midfield trio instead. We would finally see more forward-flowing football from Ødegaard without him constantly dropping deep to help Skelly, because Skelly naturally plays progressive football and avoids unnecessary back passes.

Because honestly, Eze didn’t really “eze” me at all against West Ham today. He has been given enough playing time already, so it would be fair to drop him and allow Ødegaard to play alongside Skelly and Rice. 

If we're being sincere, Odegaard is far better than Eze.

comments.text:
Gratulation an den Trainer und die Mannschaft, es war keine sooo schlechte Saison und man hat nahezu das Beste aus dem vorhandenen Stamm an Spielern herausgeholt.
Auch der Abstand zu den Bayern war jetzt so eklatant groß, wie manch anderes Jahr, Sie wurden vom BVB in den zwei Spielen mehr "gekitzelt" als von anderen Teams in der Liga.
Jetzt bleibt es spannend zu sehen, welche Konturen der Kader zur neuen Saison hin annimmt und wie unser fähiger und sympathischer Trainer das Potential aus dem Team dann herausholt, gerne auch mit mehr und attraktiverem Offensivspiel.
Abwehr können Sie mittlerweile, auch ein Verdienst des Trainer, der da an den richtigen Stellschrauben gedreht hat.

comments.text:
Félicitations à vous messieurs, c'est un TRÈS JOLI POINT que vous êtes allés chercher.
Vous avez fait l'essentiel : faire tourner l'effectif, être compétitif, et pas de blessure.

Mention spéciale à Pierre Mounguengue pour sa première entrée chez les pro en groupe A.
Tu reviendra plus fort! Ne t'enfais pas petit, c'est le métier qui rentre. Il vaut mieux que ces erreurs soient réalisées maintenant, que lors de match décisifs (ex: finale Gambardella pour toi).
C'est aussi valable pour l'équipe A de base, il vaut mieux faire les "barres, poteaux, loupés, mauvaise passe..", maintenant, et qu'elles sortent de votre système,  et être chirurgicaux de précision et d'efficacité, mercredi face au Bayern Munich.

Que le Seigneur Jésus vous bénisse et soit glorifié. ✝️

De la part d'une spectatrice.

Dieser Schrottfußball tut in den Augen weh und hat nichts mit Borussia Dortmund am Hut. Die Kosten des Kaders und die spielerische Qualität stehen in keinerlei Verhältnis.

Aber es wundert mich nicht, wenn wir nach einem Spiel gegen Freiburgs B-Elf plötzlich alles schönreden. Darin sind wir unangefochtener Meister. 

Ich vermisse den Spaß am Fußball und die Wow-Momente, für die wir mal standen. Diese Spielweise raubt mir den Spaß an meinem Verein.

Wir straucheln gegen Topgegner in der Liga und spielen gegen Abstiegskandidaten schlechter als eben diese.
Ich stand lange hinter Kovac, aber da ist mit Ball überhaupt keine Weiterentwicklung erkennbar und das Freiburg-Spiel gegen die B-Elf der Freiburger kann nicht als Zeichen für gute Entwicklung genutzt werden
Ich glaube Ole Book weiß das und ich hoffe, dass er Mut beweist

comments.text:
I stopped watching full matches from Milan several fixtures ago, the attack has been absolutely non existent, it's really painful to watch.
Leao is an ex-football player, Pulisic season is cursed, it seems like we went back to Pioli's era of complete improvisation.
Jashari, Ricci, Nkunku, Fullkrug, Estupinan, Loftus-Cheek, de Winter... 
Bartesaghi many defensive mistakes, Saelemaekers decision making so bad...
Never recovered the defensive solidity.

And well... what can I say about the management... they share great part of the blame for not reinforcing the team when it was evident that the substitutes were really lacking in quality, I mean... were you expecting the starters to carry with the team on their shoulders the whole season??

Absolutely terrible transfer market.

comments.author: @bbh9596
comments.text:
"Nemmeno le stagioni del periodo 2014-2018 ci hanno portato così tanta insoddisfazione.
Dopo 23 anni in cui guardo ogni singola partita di questo club, hanno iniziato a farci perdere la pazienza; per la prima volta ho cominciato a non guardare la partita fino alla fine. Ci hanno fatto schifo: nemmeno un tiro in porta in 90 minuti contro una squadra come il Sassuolo, che non lotta per nulla, mentre al nostro club serve blindare la posizione che porta in Champions League. Un gioco terribile... siamo in questa posizione solo grazie agli errori delle squadre avversarie, una stagione tremenda.
​I tifosi sono calmi, non c'è nessuna organizzazione, nessuna protesta di massa; la dirigenza fa quello che vuole.
Continuano con Allegri... continuate, continuate pure, finché non registrerete le umiliazioni più grandi. Tutti vedono che un allenatore del genere non può essere confermato, ma voi continuate pure, dato che siete dei 'veri esperti'.
​Vergogna, vergogna, vergogna!"

comments.text: Robbie. The reassuring, sensible voice of reason. Sticking to his Guns.

comments.author: @gooner-786
comments.text:
Arteta gave our team “ATTACKERS’ VIAGRA” 😄
This felt like Arsenal 2023 at our attacking best. Beautiful, fast, incisive passing, proper movement, through balls finally being played, and chances being created consistently. Genuinely a joy to watch.

Saka made a massive difference – everything on that right side had purpose again. And Gyökeres, what a performance. Two goals and easily his best game in an Arsenal shirt so far.
But the biggest improvement was MLS in midfield. Man of the match for me. Brave, forward, adventurous passing – more incisive than what we’ve seen from Zubimendi or Ødegaard in months.

We’ve been waiting for this for a long time. Absolutely delighted. Now the key is sustaining this level – because this is the standard.

comments.author: @Masadu6
comments.text:
Long comment : 
I have been a supporter of Real Madrid for over 35 years, and I can honestly say that I have never seen a dressing room as disappointing and lacking in character as the current one.

What makes it even more concerning is that within a single calendar year, this dressing room has effectively pushed out three managers, Carlo Ancelotti, Xabi Alonso, and by the end of the season, even Álvaro Arbeloa. That says a lot about the environment inside the team.

There seems to be a growing sense of entitlement among several players, as if they believe they have already reached the level of legends like Cristiano Ronaldo or Karim Benzema, when in reality, they are still far from it. Being a true Madridista is about commitment, sacrifice, and fighting spirit. 
Today, only Dani Carvajal consistently represents those values, while Federico Valverde is one of the few younger players who still shows the hunger, determination, and willingness to give everything on the pitch.

It is also important to be honest about the role of Florentino Pérez. 
By continuing to reward underperformance with financial comfort, the club risks encouraging complacency instead of building a team of fighters who would give everything for the badge. 
In doing so, the very identity of the club is being weakened.

The situation with Vinícius Júnior is also concerning. 
Since the moment the club publicly supported him by not attending the Ballon d’Or ceremony, regardless of the reasoning, it seems to have negatively affected his focus and attitude. 
At the same time, there are worrying signs from others, such as Jude Bellingham appearing distracted by off-field activities, reminiscent of how Gareth Bale was often criticized for prioritizing golf/cricket.

Overall, it feels like not everyone is fully committed. 
Florentino Pérez has turned Real Madrid into a highly successful business machine, but in the process, the essence of what made this club truly special may be fading (Football) .

Difficult decisions are needed. 
Leadership must change from the top, and some players should move on, in order to restore the values, identity, and fighting spirit that define this club.

This was the last derby at Goodison, the beloved home for almost all of the club's history
Tarkowski's goal left Goodison as perfectly even ground, representing the derby spectacularly 
These two clubs have been battling for over a century

If this were the last derby at Anfield, they would celebrate the same way

comments.author: @stunnermanharmless3033
comments.text:
Watching this highlight got me very angry. I mean, I was already angry that we lost, but right now, I am angry at the set of fans and analysts that push for Rashfords exit. I mean, that guy played his heart and all out. Him not getting a goal was no different from any other player on the field that day.  He didn't waste any big chance like they all claimed. The ball just didn't get into the back of the net. The entire game just didn’t go our way, that's it.

And as for Hansi Flick, I tend to love that man more every day. His mentality, his decipline, everything. So, my suggestion right now is that he starts Cancelo on the wing with Balde behind him while Rashford plays 9. Koude looked good last time, so no worries on the right. We keep things tight, we're gonna win. At least we get a 0:2 scoreline, and then we push into extra time.

XI
Garcia
Balde, Martin, Araujo, Kounde 
Garcia, Olmo
Cancelo, Fermin, Yamal
Rashford

comments.author: @conor8274
comments.text: I love this videos but I strongly believe your interpretation of the handball offence is wrong. IFAB Q&A has stated that a goalkeeper is penalised for handling outside the penalty area if ANY PART OF THEIR HAND is touching the ball outside the penalty area; the whole ball DOES NOT need to be over the line. The only part that matters is where DSC’s hand is making contact with the ball.

Does North Korea kill children, like the alliance of the European Union, the White House and Israel? Of course not.
Does Russia kill children like the alliance of the European Union, the White House and Israel? Of course not.

Does al-Qaeda kill children, like the alliance of the European Union, the White House and Israel? Isn't that shocking?

If we use scientific observation, that is, emotionally detached and ideologically undirected, it turns out that the most criminal organization in the world consists of an alliance between three political actors: the European Union, the White House and Israel.

Which Islamic country kills children in the same way as the alliance of the European Union, the White House, and Israel?

So who is more bloodthirsty and inhuman: Islam or the West?

comments.author: @2025MLSCupChampiansInterMiami
comments.text: You should’ve added “Should Zaha get a red card” during the Miami vs charlotte match

comments.author: @d_Howard
comments.text:
On the penalty in the DC United vs Chicago Fire game at 1:44 the push in the back came first, so that takes priority.  The push was definitely a foul because it denied the defending player in red an opportunity to play the ball.  It wasn't that violent, but it accomplished the desired purpose.  The push knocked the defender out of position so he couldn't play the ball.

Bad call by the ref, especially since he had the opportunity to review it.

Might be time for the MLS to take a page from the NHL's common practice on questionable calls.  In the NHL, plays are reviewed in the "Situation Room" (also called the War Room) in the Main Office where league officials, ex‑players, and retired officials review questionable calls, especially goals.

comments.author: @SorryImCanadian
comments.text: Im mixed on the TFC red bulls one. Based on precedent I would expect that to be called a pen. However, taking into consideration  the ability of the attacker to play the ball and instead jumping. I lean towards foul, but only based on precedent.

comments.text: I don't understand the logic of Gregersen looking for a foul when he is being pulled backwards yet still leans in for the header.  Not to mention, he's close enough to where if he's not being pulled backwards by the jersey, he's wide open and likely puts a shot on goal and buries it.  That's definitely a penalty.  Defender makes absolutely no attempt to play the ball, only has a handful of Gregersen's jersey.

comments.text: Xabi Alonso has a coaching style similar to Pep Guardiola and Luis Enrique, they are the type of coaches who build their teams around a collective style of play, when 11 players are on the pitch everyone must take part in attacking and also track back to support the defense, no player is given special privileges that exempt them from defensive duties that is why Alonso failed at Real Madrid because the players there had already become accustomed to a free style of play that was very successful under Carlo Ancelotti as a result they did not feel very comfortable being restricted within a clearly defined tactical system under Alonso therefore if Liverpool want to bring Alonso in to replace Arne Slot the club would have to let Salah leave because he certainly would not fit Alonso’s tactical system, Salah is already getting older, so it would be difficult for him to both contribute in attack and consistently track back to support the defense.
